opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: Every joint in my body is throbbing, my face is burning up, and I'm too exhausted to even get out of bed.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: My lupus flared after a bit of sun yesterday — my face has a burning rash, my joints are swollen, and I'm wiped out; I'm on my own.

symptom_narrative.onset: escalated over 24 hours after brief sun exposure
symptom_narrative.character: burning malar (cheek) rash, symmetric swollen painful joints, crushing fatigue
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: joints throbbing, face burning, total shutdown
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: cheeks/nose bridge (sparing folds), both wrists, knuckles, and knees
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: low-grade feverish feeling and marked photosensitivity
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I was recently diagnosed with SLE and this is a bad flare

family_history_as_reported.narrative: some autoimmune disease in the family

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: I live alone and can't use my hands to prepare food

communication_style.typical_message_style: describes the malar rash and symmetric arthritis clearly, fatigued

objective_data_offered.value: bright red raised rash across both cheeks and nose bridge, sparing the folds; both wrists/knuckles and knees swollen
my breathing is normal, no chest pain
